ddls:
CREATE TABLE "operations" (
  "op_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each operation */,
  "op_type_id" INTEGER /* ID of the operation type */,
  "sample_type_id" INTEGER /* ID of the sample type */,
  "op_count" INTEGER /* Number of operations performed */,
  "byte_count" INTEGER /* Total byte count of the operations */,
  "avg_res_time" REAL /* Average response time for the operations */,
  "avg_proc_time" REAL /* Average processing time for the operations */,
  "throughput" REAL /* Throughput of the operations */,
  "bandwidth" REAL /* Bandwidth used by the operations */,
  "succ_ratio" TEXT /* Success ratio of the operations */,
  "timestamp" TEXT /* Timestamp of the operation */,
  "region" TEXT /* Geographical region where the operation was executed */,
  "data_center" TEXT /* Specific data center where the operation was executed */,
  "status" TEXT /* Status of the operation (e.g., success, failure, in progress) */,
  "error_code" TEXT /* Error code if the operation fails */,
  "error_message" TEXT /* Detailed error message if the operation fails */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user who initiated the operation */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("op_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_operations_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_operations_op_type_id FOREIGN KEY ("op_type_id") REFERENCES operation_types ("op_type_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_operations_sample_type_id FOREIGN KEY ("sample_type_id") REFERENCES sample_types ("sample_type_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "operation_types" (
  "op_type_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each operation type */,
  "op_type_name" TEXT /* Name of the operation type (e.g., prepare, execute) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the operation type */,
  "is_deprecated" INTEGER /* Indicates if the operation type is deprecated */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp of when the operation type was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp of the last update to the operation type */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("op_type_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "sample_types" (
  "sample_type_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each sample type */,
  "sample_type_name" TEXT /* Name of the sample type (e.g., write, read) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the sample type */,
  "is_deprecated" INTEGER /* Indicates if the sample type is deprecated */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp of when the sample type was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp of the last update to the sample type */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("sample_type_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "performance_metrics" (
  "metric_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each performance metric */,
  "op_id" INTEGER /* ID of the operation associated with the metric */,
  "metric_name" TEXT /* Name of the performance metric (e.g., response time, processing time) */,
  "value" REAL /* Value of the performance metric */,
  "metric_type" TEXT /* Type of the metric (e.g., time-based, throughput, success ratio) */,
  "unit" TEXT /* Unit of the metric (e.g., seconds, bytes per second) */,
  "timestamp" TEXT /* Timestamp of when the metric was recorded */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("metric_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_performance_metrics_op_id FOREIGN KEY ("op_id") REFERENCES operations ("op_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "throughputs" (
  "throughput_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each throughput */,
  "op_id" INTEGER /* ID of the operation associated with the throughput */,
  "throughput_value" REAL /* Value of the throughput */,
  "unit" TEXT /* Unit of the throughput (e.g., bytes per second) */,
  "timestamp" TEXT /* Timestamp of when the throughput was recorded */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("throughput_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_throughputs_op_id FOREIGN KEY ("op_id") REFERENCES operations ("op_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "bandwidths" (
  "bandwidth_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each bandwidth */,
  "op_id" INTEGER /* ID of the operation associated with the bandwidth */,
  "bandwidth_value" REAL /* Value of the bandwidth */,
  "unit" TEXT /* Unit of the bandwidth (e.g., bits per second) */,
  "timestamp" TEXT /* Timestamp of when the bandwidth was recorded */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("bandwidth_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_bandwidths_op_id FOREIGN KEY ("op_id") REFERENCES operations ("op_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "success_ratios" (
  "success_ratio_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each success ratio */,
  "op_id" INTEGER /* ID of the operation associated with the success ratio */,
  "success_ratio_value" TEXT /* Value of the success ratio */,
  "unit" TEXT /* Unit of the success ratio (e.g., percentage) */,
  "timestamp" TEXT /* Timestamp of when the success ratio was recorded */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("success_ratio_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_success_ratios_op_id FOREIGN KEY ("op_id") REFERENCES operations ("op_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., engineer, operations, customer support) */,
  "password_hash" TEXT /* Secure hash of the user's password */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp of when the user account was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp of the last update to the user account */,
  "last_login" TEXT /* Timestamp of the last login */,
  "is_active" INTEGER /* Indicates if the user account is active */,
  "department" TEXT /* Department the user belongs to */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)
